factors.summary: Product quality and safety are stable, with no notable incidents or recalls mentioned.

evidence.text: Throughout COVID-19, we engaged in a number of cost savings measures in order to help mitigate the adverse effects of COVID-19 on our financial results, including certain realignment activities. We will continue to evaluate additional cost savings measures in order to reduce the impact of COVID-19 on our financial results, including the 2023 Realignment Program, and we will continue to adapt our operations to respond to the changing conditions as needed but we expect these actions to reduce as the adverse impacts of COVID-19 continue to decrease in 2023.

factors.summary: Management expects continued growth with some headwinds, including supply chain and labor issues, but remains optimistic about the outlook.

evidence.text: As the world continues to recover from COVID-19, we have seen an inflection in our served end-markets as commodity prices and mobility levels increase. With our increased backlog and improved market environment, we expect to return to growth in 2023; however, the combined effects of the supply chain, logistics and labor availability headwinds are expected to continue in 2023.

factors.summary: Market share is stable, with the company maintaining its competitive position through strong aftermarket services and strategic investments.

evidence.text: Our reputation of our product portfolio is built on more than 50 well-respected brand names such as Worthington, IDP, Valtek, Limitorque, Durco, Argus, Edward and Durametallic, which we believe to be one of the most comprehensive in the industry. factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are stable, with the company maintaining a strong market position through differentiation and strategic initiatives.

evidence.text: Our reputation of our product portfolio is built on more than 50 well-respected brand names such as Worthington, IDP, Valtek, Limitorque, Durco, Argus, Edward and Durametallic, which we believe to be one of the most comprehensive in the industry. factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include a strong brand presence, comprehensive product portfolio, and a global network of Quick Response Centers.

factors.summary: The company anticipates a slight increase in capital expenditures, primarily directed towards strategic initiatives and cost reduction measures. This indicates confidence in future growth opportunities.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures during the six months ended June 30, 2023 were $31.9 million, an increase of $0.9 million as compared with the same period in 2022. In 2023, we currently estimate capital expenditures to be between $75 million and $85 million before consideration of any acquisition activity.

The increase in SG&A was primarily due to higher broad-based annual incentive compensation, increased charges related to our Realignment Programs, $4.8 million increase in research and development costs and a $2.9 million impairment of a licensing intangible as compared to the same period in 2022.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are showing mixed trends across different segments of the company. While some divisions report normalization due to improved supply chain efficiency and lower inflationary pressures, others indicate an increase in inventory levels, potentially signaling supply chain strain or a strategic buildup.

evidence.text: Increases in inventory used $99.2 million and $96.7 million of cash flow for the six months ended June 30, 2023 and June 30, 2022, respectively. Inventory turns were 3.2 times at June 30, 2023, as compared to 3.3 times as of June 30, 2022.
The favorable impact of previously implemented sales price increases, lower supply chain inflationary pressure [...]

Despite supply chain disruption caused by COVID-19, we continue to enhance our global supply chain capabilities to increase our ability to meet global customer demands and improve the quality and timely delivery of our products over the long-term. Additionally, we continue to devote resources to improve the supply chain processes across our business segments and find areas of synergy and cost reduction, all along improving our supply chain management capability to meet global customer demands.

sentiment.label: neutral

The increase in customer bookings was driven by increased customer orders in the oil and gas, general, water management and power generation industries, partially offset by the chemical industry.

factors.summary: The company has implemented cost reduction and efficiency programs that have resulted in measurable savings. These initiatives are expected to generate annual savings of $16 million by 2024, with $3 million already achieved in the first half of 2023. Additionally, restructuring efforts contributed to improved margins by addressing financial exposures, such as the $3.5 million charge in Russia.

evidence.text: The increase in gross profit margin was primarily due to [...] a $3.5 million charge taken in the first quarter of 2022 related to our financial exposure in Russia that did not recur.

Based on 2023 Realignment Program activities initiated to date, we estimate that we have recognized cost savings of approximately $3 million during the six months ended June 30, 2023. Upon completion of the 2023 Realignment Program activities that have been identified and initiated to date, we expect full year run-rate savings of approximately $16 million in 2024.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction programs are yielding savings and improving margins.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving across the company, driven by initiatives such as lean manufacturing and six sigma, as well as operational efficiency gains from a favorable original equipment mix and reduced supply chain inflationary pressures. However, there are some offsetting factors, including higher compensation costs and increased charges related to realignment programs.

evidence.text: The goal of the CIP initiative, which includes lean manufacturing, six sigma business management strategy and value engineering, is to maximize service fulfillment to customers through on-time delivery, reduced cycle time and quality at the highest internal productivity.

The increase in gross profit margin was primarily due to the favorable impact of previously implemented sales price increases, favorable original equipment mix and lower supply chain inflationary pressure, partially offset by higher broad-based annual incentive compensation and increased charges related to our Realignment Programs as compared to the same period in 2022.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving productivity and efficiency driven by lean manufacturing and other initiatives.

factors.summary: Management exhibits mixed confidence, with increased investments in R&D and higher SG&A costs indicating a positive outlook, while cautious optimism and focus on cost savings suggest ongoing concerns due to the lingering effects of COVID-19.

We will continue to evaluate additional cost savings measures in order to reduce the impact of COVID-19 on our financial results, including the 2023 Realignment Program, and we will continue to adapt our operations to respond to the changing conditions as needed but we expect these actions to reduce as the adverse impacts of COVID-19 continue to decrease in 2023.

factors.summary: The company faces near-term risks primarily from foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, particularly in regions like the Middle East, Europe, and Latin America. Additionally, the strong U.S. dollar is creating challenges in international pricing and continues to impact supply chain disruptions and labor availability, though these latter issues have shown improvement compared to previous years.

evidence.text: The net change was primarily due to the foreign currency exchange rate movements in the United Arab Emirates dirham, Hungarian forint, Euro, and Brazilian real in relation to the U.S. dollar during the three months ended June 30, 2023, as compared with the same period in 2022.

During the first six months of 2023, COVID-related supply chain, logistics and labor availability impacts decreased when compared to 2022. The strong U.S. dollar has made and may continue to make our products more expensive overseas and has made it challenging to meet our international customers’ pricing expectations.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign currency fluctuations and supply chain challenges pose risks.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is focused on expanding its presence through investments in manufacturing, R&D, and global expansion. A key component of this strategy is the aftermarket services business, which is supported by a network of QRCs and is generally a higher margin business compared to the original equipment business.

evidence.text: FCD has a total of 44 manufacturing facilities and QRCs in 22 countries around the world, [...] Does the company believe that FCD is the second largest industrial valve supplier on a global basis.

The aftermarket service and solutions business, which is primarily served by our network of 159 QRCs located around the globe, provides a variety of service offerings for our customers including spare parts, service solutions, product life cycle solutions and other value-added services. It is generally a higher margin business compared to our original equipment business and a key component of our business strategy.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Focus on expansion, R&D, and aftermarket services supports long-term growth.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign exchange movements, commodity prices, and geopolitical factors such as sanctions impacting supply chains.

evidence.text: The effective tax rate varied from the U.S. federal statutory rate for the three months ended June 30, 2023 primarily due to the net impact of foreign operations [...] The effective tax rate varied from the U.S. federal statutory rate for the six months ended June 30, 2023 primarily due to the net impact of foreign operations and state income taxes.

The strong U.S. dollar has made and may continue to make our products more expensive overseas and has made it challenging to meet our international customers’ pricing expectations. We continue to leverage our QRC network to be positioned as near to customers as possible for service and support in order to capture valuable aftermarket business. In response to the ongoing military conflict in Ukraine, several countries, including the United States, have imposed economic sanctions and export controls on certain industry sectors and parties in Russia.

sentiment.label: neutral

factors.summary: The economic conditions are generally positive, with favorable demand in key industries and regions, though there are notable challenges such as supply chain disruptions and labor shortages.

evidence.text: The increase in customer bookings was driven by increased customer orders in the oil and gas, power generation and general industries, partially offset by decreased customer orders in the chemical and water management industries.

As the world continues to recover from COVID-19, we have seen an inflection in our served end-markets as commodity prices and mobility levels increase. With our increased backlog and improved market environment, we expect to return to growth in 2023; however, the combined effects of the supply chain, logistics and labor availability headwinds are expected to continue in 2023.
evidence.subsection: Item 2. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The economic conditions are generally positive with favorable demand, despite challenges like supply chain issues.

factors.summary: The pending acquisition of Velan Inc. is a significant catalyst expected to improve near-term results, while the completion of the 2023 Realignment Program is anticipated to drive further efficiency and growth. However, the timing of the Velan acquisition and the Realignment Program activities is uncertain due to pending regulatory approvals.

evidence.text: $6.0 million of expense related to the pending acquisition of Velan Inc. as compared to the same period in 2022.

On February 9, 2023 the Company entered into a definitive agreement under which it will acquire all of the outstanding equity of Velan Inc., a manufacturer of highly engineered industrial valves, in an all cash transaction valued at approximately $245 million. The transaction remains subject to customary closing conditions, including applicable regulatory approvals. All such regulatory approvals have been obtained, other than French Foreign Investment Screening approvals. The timing of both such approval and the close of the transaction are currently uncertain. The 2023 Realignment Program activities will be identified and initiated in phases throughout 2023.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The pending acquisition and Realignment Program are catalysts for growth, though there are regulatory uncertainties.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing negative impacts from higher interest rates, specifically increased interest expenses, which are creating margin pressure. However, the company maintains a strong liquidity position and has implemented cost-saving measures to manage financial risks associated with these interest rate changes.

evidence.text: Interest expense for the three months ended June 30, 2023 increased $5.5 million, as compared with the same period in 2022, primarily due to higher effective interest rates on our outstanding debt resulting in part to the termination of cross-currency swap agreements in the 4th quarter of 2022.

We do not currently anticipate, nor are we aware of, any significant market conditions or commitments that would change any of our conclusions of the liquidity currently available to us. We expect the liquidity discussed above coupled with the costs savings measures planned and already in place will further enable us to maintain adequate liquidity over the short-term (next 12 months) and long-term (beyond the next 12 months).
evidence.subsection: Item 2. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher interest rates are increasing expenses and pressuring margins, despite cost-saving measures.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are creating both opportunities and risks, particularly in regions with volatile currencies.

evidence.text: The net change was primarily due to the foreign currency exchange rate movements in the United Arab Emirates dirham, Hungarian forint, Euro, and Brazilian real in relation to the U.S. dollar during the three months ended June 30, 2023, as compared with the same period in 2022.
The strong U.S. dollar has made and may continue to make our products more expensive overseas and has made it challenging to meet our international customers’ pricing expectations.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements are creating risks, particularly with a strong U.S. dollar affecting pricing.

factors.summary: The company faces significant geopolitical risks, primarily from changes in tax laws due to OECD initiatives and the ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict. These risks include potential double taxation, reduced tax incentives, operational disruptions, and financial impacts, though the latter has not been material to the overall business.

evidence.text: In December 2022, the European Union ('EU') member states reached an agreement to implement the minimum tax component [...] Some of these legislative changes could result in double taxation of our non-U.S. earnings, a reduction in the tax benefit received from our tax incentives, or other impacts to our effective tax rate and tax liabilities.

In response to the ongoing military conflict in Ukraine, several countries, including the United States, have imposed economic sanctions and export controls on certain industry sectors and parties in Russia. As a result of this conflict, including the aforementioned sanctions and overall instability in the region, in February 2022 we stopped accepting new orders in Russia and temporarily suspended fulfillment of existing orders. In March 2022, we made the decision to permanently cease all Company operations in Russia.
evidence.subsection: Impact of Russia-Ukraine Conflict on our Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant geopolitical risks from tax changes and conflict pose challenges, though not material yet.

Despite supply chain disruption caused by COVID-19, we continue to enhance our global supply chain capabilities to increase our ability to meet global customer demands and improve the quality and timely delivery of our products over the long-term.

factors.summary: The company is undergoing headcount restructuring as part of the 2023 Realignment Program, which is affecting SG&A expenses. The restructuring is aimed at achieving cost savings and improving efficiency, with some initial cost savings recognized. However, the ongoing activities are also leading to increased charges and investments, including non-cash expenses.

evidence.text: The increase in SG&A was primarily due to higher broad-based annual incentive compensation, increased charges related to our Realignment Programs, $4.8 million increase in research and development costs and a $2.9 million impairment of a licensing intangible as compared to the same period in 2022.

The 2023 Realignment Program activities will be identified and initiated in phases throughout 2023. We currently anticipate a total investment in realignment activities that have been identified and initiated to date of approximately $40 million of which $13 million is estimated to be non-cash. Based on 2023 Realignment Program activities initiated to date, we estimate that we have recognized cost savings of approximately $3 million during the six months ended June 30, 2023.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring is aimed at efficiency but includes costs and investments, with mixed impacts on expenses.

factors.summary: Construction activity and infrastructure spending are growing, driving demand for the company's products in key regions, particularly in industries like oil and gas, water management, and power generation.

evidence.text: Increased customer sales were driven by both aftermarket and customer original equipment sales. The increase was primarily driven by increased customer sales of $22.9 million into North America, $4.7 million into Africa, $9.6 million into the Middle East, $6.9 million into Europe and $2.2 million into Latin America.
The increase in customer bookings was driven by increased customer orders in the oil and gas, general, water management and power generation industries, partially offset by the chemical industry.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Construction and infrastructure spending is growing, driving demand for products in key industries.

factors.summary: Equipment replacement cycles are accelerating, driven by increased orders in key industries and aftermarket service demand from the installed base.

evidence.text: The increase in customer bookings was driven by increased customer orders in the oil and gas, power generation and general industries, partially offset by decreased customer orders in the chemical and water management industries.

The worldwide installed base of our products is an important source of aftermarket revenue, where products are relied upon to maximize operating time of many key industrial processes. We continue to invest significantly in our aftermarket strategy to provide local support to drive customer investments in our offerings and use of our services to replace or repair installed products.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Accelerating equipment replacement cycles and increased aftermarket demand indicate growth opportunities.

factors.summary: The aftermarket service revenue is growing and contributing to overall revenue growth. It is growing faster than new equipment sales, driven by a strong installed base and increasing demand for value-added services. However, the percentage of aftermarket sales slightly decreased from 53% to 52% of total sales compared to the same period in 2022. Despite this, aftermarket bookings were a key driver of customer bookings increase.

evidence.text: Increased customer sales were driven by both aftermarket and customer original equipment sales.

Aftermarket sales represented approximately 52% of total sales, as compared with approximately 53% of total sales for the same period in 2022. The increase in customer bookings was driven by aftermarket bookings.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Aftermarket service revenue is growing, contributing to overall revenue growth despite a slight decrease in percentage.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing, driven by higher sales volumes and production needs, as well as higher demand and improved supply chain efficiency. The evidence highlights growth across various regions and ongoing efforts to enhance supply chain capabilities.

evidence.text: Sales for the three months ended June 30, 2023 increased by $150.5 million, or 24.5%, as compared with the same period in 2022. [...] Increased customer sales of $13.0 million into Asia Pacific, $25.4 million into Europe, $52.7 million into North America, $53.6 million into the Middle East and $14.9 million into Latin America were partially offset by decreased sales of $8.2 million into Africa.

We continue to enhance our global supply chain capabilities to increase our ability to meet global customer demands and improve the quality and timely delivery of our products over the long-term. Additionally, we continue to devote resources to improve the supply chain processes across our business segments and find areas of synergy and cost reduction, all along improving our supply chain management capability to meet global customer demands.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capacity utilization and supply chain improvements suggest operational efficiency and growth.

factors.summary: R&D investment is increasing, reflecting a commitment to innovation and product development, as well as initiatives to enhance product offerings and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: The increase in SG&A was primarily due to higher broad-based annual incentive compensation, increased charges related to our Realignment Programs, $4.8 million increase in research and development costs and a $2.9 million impairment of a licensing intangible as compared to the same period in 2022.
A $7.3 million increase in research and development costs.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Higher R&D investment indicates a commitment to innovation and product development.
